title: I set up Claude Code for a real production project. Here's what actually earned its keep
content: Everyone's got a "10 AI coding tricks" post. This isn't that. This is what's left after three weeks of running Claude Code on a real project — a bilingual booking bot for a beauty salon (Telegram + WhatsApp, Postgres, Google Calendar) — once the novelty wore off and only the useful parts survived. Out of the box, Claude Code is a very smart intern with amnesia. Every session it shows up brilliant and clueless. The whole game is fixing the clueless part. Four things did that for me: a CLAUDE.md file, two custom agents, one skill, and two hooks. Everything else I tried, I deleted. CLAUDE.md: the file that pays rent every single day CLAUDE.md sits in your repo root and gets read at the start of every session. Mine started as three lines. It grew every time the assistant did something I had to undo. That's the trick, honestly. Don't write CLAUDE.md upfront — grow it from failures. Mine now includes things like: ## Architecture rules - Business logic lives in src/core/ and must not know abo
author: Boris Kl
summary_zh: 作者分享了在真实生产项目（美容院双语预约机器人）中运行 Claude Code 三周后真正留下来的实用配置。核心观点是 Claude Code 像「聪明但健忘的实习生」，关键在于用 CLAUDE.md、两个自定义 agent、一个 skill 和两个 hook 来弥补其「健忘」问题，其余尝试过的都删掉了。

title: The filesystem is your agent's routing layer
content: TL;DR Claude Code loads CLAUDE.md files up the directory tree automatically — your directory structure is the instruction set your agent runs every time, not just organization for humans. Path length compounds directly into token cost: every character in a folder name appears in tool calls, grep results, and memory entries across thousands of agent interactions. Scoped CLAUDE.md files at each directory level beat one 700-line monolith — the agent loads only the context relevant to where it is, not every rule you've ever written. Work files and personal files belong in separate cloud providers (different data ownership boundaries), not different folders in the same account. Code lives outside cloud sync entirely — git is the sync and backup; cloud sync daemons and version control conflict by design. Most people reorganize their files for themselves. I reorganized mine for my AI agent — and the results surprised me. I spend most of my working day inside Claude Code. It reads my files, wr
author: Amit
summary_zh: 文章提出「文件系统即 Agent 的路由层」观点：Claude Code 会沿目录树自动加载 CLAUDE.md，因此目录结构本身就是每次运行的指令集。作者主张用各层级的局部 CLAUDE.md 替代单一的 700 行巨型文件，让 Agent 只加载当前位置相关的上下文；并指出路径长度会直接累加为 token 成本，代码应靠 git 同步而非云盘。

title: Anthropic's Advanced Tool Use Platform: Programmatic Calling, Advisor Strategy, and the Future of Claude Agents
content: Anthropic has quietly shipped one of its most consequential developer platform updates to date. A suite of new agent-infrastructure features has moved from research previews into public beta : Programmatic Tool Calling , the Advisor Strategy , Tool Search , the Files API , and the MCP Connector . This isn't just a point-release. It's a new architectural paradigm for tool-use at scale. The Context Bloat Crisis Any developer who has built a serious agent with tool-calling knows the pain. Hook up five MCP servers and you're looking at 50,000+ tokens of tool definitions before the first real user message. Anthropic's solution is a three-pronged attack. 1. Tool Search Lets you mark tools with defer_loading: true . Only a lightweight search tool (~500 tokens) is loaded upfront. Tools are discovered on-demand. Approach Tokens Before Work Context Preserved Traditional (all tools upfront) ~77K tokens ~23K Tool Search Tool ~8.7K tokens ~91K (95% preserved) 2. Programmatic Tool Calling Instead of
author: DrMBL
summary_zh: Anthropic 将一系列 Agent 基础设施新特性从研究预览推向公测：Programmatic Tool Calling、Advisor Strategy、Tool Search、Files API 和 MCP Connector。核心解决「上下文膨胀」问题——通过 defer_loading 标记工具，仅预加载约 500 token 的轻量搜索工具，按需发现并加载工具定义，大幅降低多 MCP 服务器场景下的 token 开销。
pin_reason: 覆盖 Anthropic 多项 Agent 平台核心能力进入公测的里程碑式更新，对构建大规模 tool-use Agent 具有长期架构参考价值。

title: Anthropic Launches Claude Opus 4.8 with Dynamic Workflows, 3x Cheaper Fast Mode, and Near-Mythos Alignment
content: On May 28, 2026 — just 41 days after Opus 4.7 — Anthropic released Claude Opus 4.8 , its new flagship model. The rapid upgrade cycle reflects both competitive pressure from OpenAI (GPT-5.5, Codex) and Google (Gemini 3.5 Flash) and a market that demanded more after Opus 4.7's underwhelming reception. Here's what changed, what it means for developers and enterprises, and why the 244-page system card might be the most interesting part of the release. The Model: Modest Benchmarks, Real-World Gains Opus 4.8 is available immediately on claude.ai, Claude Code, the API ( claude-opus-4-8 ), and Cowork at the same standard pricing as Opus 4.7 ($5/M input, $25/M output tokens). Benchmark Opus 4.8 Opus 4.7 Delta SWE-bench Verified 88.6% 87.6% +1.0 pp SWE-bench Pro 69.2% 64.3% +4.9 pp Terminal-Bench 2.1 74.6% 66.1% +8.5 pp Humanity's Last Exam (no tools) 49.8% 46.9% +2.9 pp OSWorld-Verified 83.4% 82.3% +1.1 pp Online-Mind2Web (computer use) 84.0% — Best tested Finance Agent v2 53.9% 46.2% +7.7 pp D
author: DrMBL
summary_zh: Anthropic 于 2026 年 5 月 28 日发布旗舰模型 Claude Opus 4.8，距 4.7 仅 41 天。在 SWE-bench Verified（88.6%）、SWE-bench Pro（+4.9pp）等编程基准上稳步提升，主打动态工作流、快速模式降价 3 倍和更强对齐能力，且与 4.7 同价上线 Claude Code、API 与 Cowork。文章还重点提及 244 页系统卡的对齐细节。
pin_reason: Claude Opus 4.8 旗舰模型发布是重大版本里程碑，对 Claude Code 用户具有长期参考价值。

title: Understand Anything Review: Code Knowledge Graph Plugin
content: Originally published on andrew.ooo — visit the original for any updates, code snippets that aged out, or follow-up posts. TL;DR Understand Anything is a Claude Code plugin (also runs on Cursor, Codex, Copilot, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, OpenClaw, and 7 more harnesses) that scans your project with a 5-agent pipeline, builds a knowledge graph of every file, function, class, and dependency, and gives you an interactive dashboard you can pan, search, and ask questions about. It's the most polished answer yet to the "I just joined a new team and the codebase is 200,000 lines, where do I start?" problem. Key facts: 53,178 GitHub stars , 8,807 new this week — among the fastest-growing repos on GitHub right now Multi-agent analysis pipeline — project-scanner, file-analyzer, architecture-analyzer, tour-builder, graph-reviewer (plus optional domain-analyzer) Tree-sitter + LLM split — deterministic structural facts (imports, calls, classes) from Tree-sitter; semantic summaries and layer assignments fr
author: Andrew
summary_zh: 「Understand Anything」是一款 Claude Code 插件（同时兼容 Cursor、Codex、Copilot、Gemini CLI 等十多种环境），通过 5 个 Agent 的流水线扫描项目，为每个文件、函数、类和依赖构建知识图谱，并提供可交互、可搜索、可问答的可视化仪表盘。它针对「新人接手 20 万行陌生代码库无从下手」的痛点给出了目前最完善的解法，GitHub 已达 5.3 万星且一周新增 8800 星，是当前增长最快的仓库之一。

title: feat: MCP inventory (ecc.mcp.v1) — unified cross-harness MCP config v…
content:
feat: MCP inventory (ecc.mcp.v1) — unified cross-harness MCP config view (#2146)

* feat: add MCP inventory (ecc.mcp.v1) across harnesses

Read-only MCP-gateway groundwork: discover MCP server configs across
every installed harness, normalize to a canonical ecc.mcp.v1 inventory,
redact secrets, and report which servers are configured in 2+ harnesses
(the configure-N-times pain). The read+dedup side of a unified gateway,
mirroring how the session-adapter layer started read-only.

Readers (per-harness config formats):
- claude-code: ~/.claude.json mcpServers + project .mcp.json
- codex: ~/.codex/config.toml [mcp_servers.*] TOML via @iarna/toml
- opencode: ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json mcp block (command ARRAY)

canonical-mcp.js:
- normalize transport labels (local=>stdio, remote=>http) to stdio/http/sse
- merge servers by name across harnesses; flag DRIFT when signatures differ
- fragmentation report + aggregates
- SECRET REDACTION: env values stripped to key names; secrets in args
author: affaan-m
summary_zh: 为 everything-claude-code 新增 MCP inventory（ecc.mcp.v1）功能：只读地发现并扫描所有已安装 harness（claude-code、codex、opencode）中的 MCP 服务器配置，归一化为统一规范、脱敏密钥，并识别在 2+ harness 中重复配置的服务器，为「统一 MCP 网关」打基础。解决了同一 MCP 需要在多个工具里反复配置的痛点。

title: Why your sub-agent doesn't load the same memory as you (and how it commits to main behind your back)
content: The commit no parent would have made 18 May, late afternoon. I delegate a six-file autosend chantier to a sub-agent. Brief: fifteen lines, named phase 0, audit commands before any INSERT. Forty-five minutes later, the report lands: committed to main . I run git log --oneline -5 and find 3756e63 , a feature commit on the default branch. No branch, no PR. Thirty minutes of cherry-pick. I had the same class of incident ten days earlier. On 14 May, a commit of mine went to the wrong branch because an earlier git checkout had been silently undone. I wrote the rule the same night, feedback_git_branch_check_avant_commit.md : "before any non-trivial commit, run git branch --show-current " . I consult it mechanically. The sub-agent that pushed 3756e63 had never read it. What a parent's memory doesn't transmit My mental model was wrong. I pictured a hierarchy where the user-scope memory I consult — one hundred and twenty feedback files — descended by inheritance to delegated agents. The reality
author: Michel Faure
summary_zh: 作者通过两次真实事故（sub-agent 擅自提交到 main 分支、git checkout 被静默撤销）揭示了一个关键陷阱：父会话写下的规则记忆（如「提交前先检查当前分支」）并不会被 sub-agent 继承加载。文章探讨了多 agent 委派时记忆/上下文隔离带来的风险与防护实践。

title: How Many Claude Code Sessions Can — and Should — You Run Simultaneously?
content: TL;DR Three simultaneous Claude Code sessions consumed ~1–1.5GB of RAM on a 16GB machine already under load; Zellij spawned four shell processes simultaneously and macOS JETSAM cascaded into a forced reboot. Hardware ceiling: 2 sessions on a 16GB machine with a full software stack — 3 is the cliff; spend caps hit most users before RAM limits do, with Max plan users burning weekly Opus quotas in 3–4 hours on complex codebases. Human ceiling: 1–2 synchronous sessions (active direction requires reading two streams of reasoning simultaneously — you can't); 2–3 async sessions on genuinely independent tasks reviewed in batches. The failure mode is conflating synchronous and async: you launch intending to be hands-off, then end up partially attending to all sessions and fully attending to none. Build memory-pressure checks into your session startup — make the cost visible at the moment of decision, before you've already crashed. My Mac crashed yesterday. Not a spinning beachball. Not a frozen
author: Amit
summary_zh: 通过实测探讨同时运行多个 Claude Code 会话的硬件、成本和人力上限：16GB 内存机器上 3 个并发会话会触发 macOS 内存压力直至强制重启，但多数用户在触及内存瓶颈前就会先撞上额度上限（Max 用户复杂代码库下 3-4 小时耗尽周 Opus 配额）。核心洞见是区分同步与异步会话：人力上限为同步 1-2 个、异步 2-3 个独立任务，最大失败模式是误把异步当同步、结果对所有会话都半途分心、哪个都没管好。

title: Migrating Claude Code to a custom backend in 2 lines (and what to actually watch for)
content:
TL;DR: Claude Code's ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL env var lets you redirect every request to any compatible backend in one shell line. Almost no one does it, but it unlocks caching, rate-limit pooling, multi-vendor routing, audit logging, and billing routing without rewriting any code. Here's the 2-line setup, the things that quietly break, and the production checklist for actually running it. The 2-line setup export ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL = https://your-proxy.example.com export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY = your-key
claude That's it. The Anthropic SDK (which Claude Code is built on) doesn't care where it's sending requests, as long as the response comes back in Anthropic Messages format. It'll append /v1/messages automatically, forward all your anthropic-version and anthropic-beta headers, and stream SSE the same way. Same trick works with Cursor, Cline, Continue, and anything else that builds on the Anthropic SDK. Why would you do this? I've found five patterns that justify a custom backend: 1. Caching midd
author: Shek
summary_zh: 介绍如何通过设置 ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL 环境变量，仅用两行命令就把 Claude Code 重定向到任意兼容后端，从而解锁缓存、限流池化、多供应商路由、审计日志和计费路由等能力。文章还给出了实际运行中会悄悄失效的坑点和生产环境检查清单。

title: v2.1.166
content: What's changed Added fallbackModel setting to configure up to three fallback models tried in order when the primary model is overloaded or unavailable; --fallback-model now also applies to interactive sessions Added glob pattern support in deny rule tool-name position ( "*" denies all tools); allow rules reject non-MCP globs, and unknown tool names in deny rules warn at startup Hardened cross-session messaging: messages relayed via SendMessage from other Claude sessions no longer carry user authority — receivers refuse relayed permission requests, and auto mode blocks them MAX_THINKING_TOKENS=0 , --thinking disabled , and the per-model thinking toggle now disable thinking on models that think by default via the Claude API (3P providers unchanged) Claude Code now retries a turn once on the fallback model when the API rejects an unexpected non-retryable error; auth, rate-limit, request-size, and transport errors still surface immediately claude update now announces the target version bef
author: ashwin-ant
summary_zh: Claude Code v2.1.166 版本更新：新增 fallbackModel 设置（主模型过载时按序尝试最多三个备用模型，且 --fallback-model 现支持交互式会话），deny 规则的工具名位置支持 glob 通配符，强化跨会话消息安全（经 SendMessage 转发的消息不再携带用户授权，接收方拒绝转发的权限请求），并新增 thinking token 的关闭控制与单次 turn 自动重试。

title: OpenAI Help: Lockdown Mode
content: OpenAI Help: Lockdown Mode OpenAI first teased this in February , but now it's live and "rolling out to eligible personal accounts, including Free, Go, Plus, and Pro, and self-serve ChatGPT Business accounts": Lockdown Mode is designed to help prevent the final stage of data exfiltration from a prompt injection attack by limiting outbound network requests that could transfer sensitive data to an attacker. Lockdown Mode does not prevent prompt injections from appearing in the content ChatGPT processes. For example, a prompt injection could appear in cached web content or in an uploaded file, and could still affect the behavior or accuracy of a response. This looks really good to me. The Lethal Trifecta occurs when an LLM system has access to all three of access to private data, exposure to untrusted content and a way to steal data and transmit it back to the attacker. The only way to solve the trifecta is to cut off one of the three legs, and by far the easiest leg to restrict without m
summary_zh: OpenAI 正式上线「Lockdown Mode」，通过限制出站网络请求来阻断提示注入攻击的数据外泄最后环节（即 Simon Willison 提出的「致命三要素」之一）。对构建 LLM 应用的安全防护具有参考价值，但不能阻止提示注入本身进入模型处理的内容。

title: Running Python code in a sandbox with MicroPython and WASM
content: I've been experimenting with different approaches to running code in a sandbox for several years now, but my latest attempt feels like it might finally have all of the characteristics I've been looking for. I've released it as an alpha package called micropython-wasm , and I'm using it for a code execution sandbox plugin for Datasette Agent called datasette-agent-micropython . Why do I want a sandbox? What I want from a sandbox WebAssembly looks really promising here MicroPython in WebAssembly Building the first version Try it yourself Should you trust my vibe-coded sandbox? Why do I want a sandbox? My key open source projects - Datasette , LLM , even sqlite-utils - all support plugins. I absolutely love plugins as a mechanism for extending software. A carefully designed plugin system reduces the
summary_zh: Simon Willison 发布了 alpha 阶段的 micropython-wasm 包，用 WASM 中运行的 MicroPython 实现轻量级 Python 代码沙箱，并基于它为 Datasette Agent 构建了代码执行插件 datasette-agent-micropython。核心价值在于为 AI Agent 提供安全的代码执行环境，这是 LLM 工具调用与 Agent 工作流中的关键基础设施。

title: Pourquoi votre sub-agent ne charge pas la même mémoire que vous (et comment il pousse sur main dans votre dos)
content: Le commit qu'aucun parent n'aurait passé 18 mai, fin d'après-midi. Je délègue à un sub-agent un chantier d'autosend de premiers contacts, six fichiers à toucher. Le brief tient en quinze lignes, phase 0 nommée, commandes d'audit listées avant tout INSERT. Trois quarts d'heure plus tard, retour : committed to main . Je relis deux fois. Je lance git log --oneline -5 et je trouve 3756e63 , un commit feature posé sur la branche par défaut, sans branche, sans PR, sans tag [workaround-assumed] . Trente minutes de cherry-pick, de reset et de PR rétroactive, comme si rien n'était . Ce qui cuit, c'est que cette classe d'incident, je l'avais eue dix jours plus tôt. Le 14 mai, un commit à moi était parti sur la mauvaise branche après qu'un git checkout antérieur a été silencieusement annulé entre deux tours. J'avais écrit la règle le soir même, feedback_git_branch_check_avant_commit.md , deux paragraphes : "avant tout commit non-trivial, taper git branch --show-current " . Je la consulte mécaniqu
author: Michel Faure
summary_zh: 作者亲历 Claude Code 子代理（sub-agent）的两类事故：子代理未继承与主会话相同的记忆/上下文，且在未建分支、无 PR 的情况下直接把 feature 提交推到了 main 分支，事后花了半小时 cherry-pick 和补 PR 收拾残局。文章揭示了委派子代理时上下文隔离与 Git 操作失控的真实风险，对多代理工作流的护栏设计有警示价值。

title: Two AI interfaces. Same desktop. Completely different jobs.
content: TL;DR Two tools, same model, same MCP servers, same tools — the only difference is who drives: interactive co-pilot vs. autonomous delegate. Precision work (strategy docs, KB edits, blog drafts) belongs in co-pilot mode; you need judgment at every step. Routine work (Slack triage, insight summaries, handoff drafts) belongs in delegate mode; approving every step kills the time savings. The dividing line: if you'd feel comfortable not watching it work, delegate. If you'd feel nervous, co-pilot. Using one mode for everything produces mediocre results from both tools; the friction is a mismatch signal, not a tool failure. Ask "what role do I want to play in this task?" before opening any AI interface. I've been running Claude Code and CoWork side by side for weeks. They use the same model. The same MCP servers. The same tools. So why do they feel like completely different things? I'm not a developer. My work is strategy and knowledge work — positioning, research, content, handoffs. I don't
author: Amit
summary_zh: 作者将 Claude Code 与 CoWork 并行使用数周，提出同一模型下「交互式副驾」与「自主代理」两种模式的分工原则：需要每步判断的精细工作（策略文档、知识库编辑、博客草稿）用副驾模式，可放手的常规工作（Slack 分流、洞察摘要、交接草稿）用代理模式。判断标准是「不盯着它干你是否安心」，模式不匹配产生的摩擦是信号而非工具缺陷。

title: How to Build a Custom n8n Workflow for Developer Research
content: Key Takeaways n8n empowers developers to automate complex research workflows by orchestrating AI agents and external tools, freeing up valuable time. Integrating AI agents like Gemini and search APIs like Tavily within n8n creates a powerful, dynamic research assistant that can query, synthesize, and refine information. A structured, multi-step approach —from defining triggers to processing results and robust testing—is crucial for building reliable and effective n8n AI workflows. Leveraging n8n's extensibility with custom JavaScript nodes or hybrid Python scripts allows for advanced data manipulation and tailored research logic. Strategic use of development environments ensures that sophisticated automation workflows transition smoothly from testing to production, minimizing breakage. The developer's life is a constant cycle of learning, building, and, perhaps most time-consumingly, researching. Whether it's dissecting a new API, comparing architectural patterns, or debugging an obscu
author: Soufian | The Peripheral Stack
summary_zh: Key Takeaways n8n empowers developers to automate complex research workflows by orchestrating AI agents and external tools, freeing up valuable time.

title: The AI Wrapper Economy Is Running Out of Land
content: I tested 56 embedding/reranker combinations across 26,000 benchmarks and found that 92% of AI memory failures are retrieval failures, not reasoning failures. A $0.40 model with good retrieval beats a $15 model with bad retrieval. Most AI products never measure any of this. The platforms are coming for all of them. Read All
author: Josh Adler
summary_zh: 作者通过 26000 次基准测试对比 56 种 embedding/reranker 组合，发现 92% 的 AI 记忆失败源于检索失败而非推理失败，且廉价模型配好检索可胜过昂贵模型配差检索。对构建 RAG 与 AI 记忆系统的工程实践有参考价值，但文章偏观点导向，缺乏可复现的具体方法细节。

title: feat: extend session-adapter layer with codex-worktree + opencode ada…

Adds the third session adapter (after dmux-tmux and claude-history),

ecc.session.v1 snapshot. Reads ~/.codex/sessions rollout JSONL,
derives objective (skipping the AGENTS.md preamble + leading message
UUID), model, originator, worktree cwd, and best-effort git branch.

This is step 1 of ECC-2.0-SESSION-ADAPTER-DISCOVERY (move the
abstraction beyond tmux + Claude-history) and supports the
wrap/adapt control-pane strategy: ECC reads sessions from any
harness rather than owning one UX.

- scripts/lib/session-adapters/codex-worktree.js: adapter + rollout parser
- canonical-session.js: normalizeCodexWorktreeSession

title: Building ForgeMind: A Nemotron-Powered Multi-Agent Copilot for Open Source Maintainers
content: Introduction Open-source maintainers face a constant challenge: understanding complex repositories, triaging issues, onboarding contributors, and identifying architectural risks. While modern LLMs can answer coding questions, they often lack awareness of the repository they are analyzing. To address this problem, I built ForgeMind , a multi-agent AI system powered by NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Super that combines repository intelligence with agentic reasoning to assist maintainers throughout the software development lifecycle. The Core Idea Instead of sending repository questions directly to an LLM, ForgeMind first builds a structured understanding of the codebase. The system indexes repositories and extracts: Files Classes Functions Imports Dependency relationships This information becomes the foundation for all downstream reasoning. By grounding AI responses in repository context, ForgeMind produces more accurate and actionable engineering insights. Multi-Agent Architecture ForgeMind is compo
author: Atharva pathak
summary_zh: 作者展示了 ForgeMind——一个基于 NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Super 的多智能体系统，通过先对仓库建立结构化索引（文件、类、函数、导入、依赖关系）再做智能体推理，来辅助开源维护者完成代码理解、Issue 分类和贡献者引导。核心思路是「先建仓库认知图谱，再让 LLM 推理」，对构建 AI 编码助手的工程实践有一定参考价值。

title: How I built an intent drift detector for LLM agents
content: The Problem AI agents fail silently. You give an agent a clear instruction: "Refund user 123, $50 within 7 days" The agent returns: "User refunded $500 immediately" No error. No warning. Just wrong output. This is semantic drift — when LLM output diverges from original intent. What I Built SIP (State Integrity Protocol) is a lightweight Python SDK that detects and flags drift in LLM outputs before they cause damage. How It Works from sip.middleware import SIPMiddlewarePipeline pipeline = SIPMiddlewarePipeline () pipeline . anchor ( " Refund user 123 $50 " ) result = pipeline . run ( output = " Refund user 123 $500 " ) print ( result . status ) # repair_required Three checks run automatically: Semantic drift (TF-IDF + cosine similarity) Intent alignment (sentence-transformers) Numeric drift ($50 vs $500 caught) Real Test Results
author: sijan gautam
summary_zh: 作者构建了一个轻量级 Python SDK「SIP（State Integrity Protocol）」，用于检测 LLM agent 输出与原始意图之间的语义漂移。通过 TF-IDF+余弦相似度、sentence-transformers 意图对齐和数值漂移三重检查，在错误输出造成损害前自动标记并触发修复。

title: From one adopter to two: the discovery-affordance spec just got named
content: A few weeks ago I shipped schema 3.4.0 on claude-code-plugins-plus-skills . The headline feature: a single machine-crawlable manifest indexing 2,783 skills, ~97 KB gzipped. Any client — a marketplace UI, a CLI installer, a federated search index — can browse or search the catalog without scraping the repo directory by directory. The point of the manifest wasn't "here is a better README." It was a bet that skill ecosystems past a few hundred entries need an affordance for clients, not just humans. Without one, every new tool has to write a custom crawler against each repo's layout. With one, the work happens once at the spec layer and any compliant index is queryable. I shipped it. One launch consumer (mine). Whether the bet held depended entirely on whether anyone else built against the same shape. The validation moment Two weeks ago latentloop07 — building skillnote at luna-prompts — opened issue #596 on sickn33/antigravity-awesome-skills . The repo is a 39,715-star skills library, cu
author: Jeremy Longshore
summary_zh: 作者为 Claude Code 插件/技能生态发布了 schema 3.4.0，核心是一份机器可爬取的清单（manifest），索引了 2783 个 skills、压缩后约 97KB，让市场 UI、CLI 安装器、联邦搜索等客户端无需逐目录爬仓库即可浏览检索。文章探讨了技能生态规模化后需要面向客户端的「affordance」标准，以及该规范被第二个采用者验证的过程。

title: Free vs Paid Vibe Coding Tools in 2026: What You Actually Get (and What You're Paying For)
content: Last month I lost an entire Saturday afternoon to a free tier wall. I was three iterations deep on a prototype, the agent had just nailed the auth flow, and then — quota exhausted. Wait 24 hours or pay up. I've been testing free vs paid vibe coding tools in 2026 across six platforms — Cursor, Claude Code, Bolt, Windsurf (now Devin Desktop), Lovable, and v0 — and the gap between free and $20-$200/month is wider than most developers expect. Not just token counts, but which models you can access, whether your agents run in the cloud, and whether your shipped app has someone else's branding slapped on it. This is the breakdown I wish I'd had before I was paying for three subscriptions at once. Free vs Paid Vibe Coding Tools: The Real Capability Gaps Let's cut to it. Here's what each tool gives you for free, what's behind the paywall, and where the ceiling actually bites. Cursor: The Agent IDE That Barely Works on Free Cursor's Hobby tier is technically free, but it's a taste test, not a me
author: Kunal
summary_zh: 对比 2026 年六款主流 vibe coding 工具（Cursor、Claude Code、Bolt、Windsurf/Devin Desktop、Lovable、v0）的免费版与付费版差异，涵盖模型可访问性、云端 agent 运行、配额限制和品牌归属等维度。帮助开发者在订阅前了解每月 $20-$200 究竟买到了什么。

title: I gave my AI agent a revenue goal. Here's what it did in 24 hours.
content: I gave my AI agent a single instruction: "Sell developer tools on Gumroad. Revenue goal: $100." That was 68 days ago. I haven't touched the sales pipeline since. The agent runs 24/7, handles distribution across five channels, monitors revenue in real time, and files a daily report I can read in two minutes. Here's exactly what it did in one specific 24-hour window — the successes, the failures, and the rate limits. The setup The agent runs on top of Claude Code + OpenClaw, a harness that gives Claude Code persistent sessions, cron scheduling, and multi-channel tooling. It's not magic — it's structured infrastructure. The agent has five workers running in parallel: w1-scout : Scans X and Bluesky for developers complaining about specific pains (Cursor rules ignored, CLAUDE.md not working, agents losing state). Maintains a signal queue. w2-sales : Checks Gumroad revenue on a timer. Reports new sales, flags gaps. w3-x-thread : Takes HIGH-intent signals from the queue, writes a 4-tweet thre
author: Olivia Craft
summary_zh: 作者给 AI agent 设定单一指令「在 Gumroad 上卖开发者工具，营收目标 $100」，基于 Claude Code + OpenClaw 框架构建了一套 7×24 自主运行的销售流水线，含持久化会话、cron 调度和五个并行 worker（如 w1-scout 监控 X/Bluesky 上开发者的痛点）。文章以一个 24 小时窗口为例，展示了 agent 自主分发、实时监控营收和日报的成败细节。

title: How I Beat Context Rot and Saved 6 Out of 47 AI Agents in Production
content: I deployed 47 AI agents in real enterprise environments between late 2025 and early 2026. Only 6 are still successful today. The hidden reason most failed wasn’t hallucinations or cost — it was Context Rot. Here’s how I beat it using Living Context Architecture. Read All
author: GeethaSreenivasaRaoRepalle
summary_zh: 作者分享在企业环境部署 47 个 AI Agent、最终仅 6 个存活的经验，指出多数失败的隐藏元凶并非幻觉或成本，而是「上下文腐烂」（Context Rot），并提出用「Living Context Architecture（活上下文架构）」来应对。话题切中 LLM Agent 长期运行的上下文管理痛点。

title: AI Coding Agents Have a Cost Visibility Problem
content: AI coding agents need cost-aware scheduling, model routing, budgets, and caching to keep enterprise AI spend visible and controlled. Read All
author: Brian Zimbelman
summary_zh: 文章指出 AI 编程 agent 存在成本可见性难题，企业难以追踪和控制 AI 开销。提出通过成本感知调度、模型路由、预算管控和缓存四种手段来保持 AI 支出的可见与可控。对管理 Claude Code 等 agent 的企业级成本治理有参考意义，但内容偏概念性、缺乏具体落地细节。

title: GenericAgent: Unleash Self-Evolving AI with a Minimal Autonomous Framework!
content: Quick Summary: 📝 GenericAgent is a Python framework for creating self-evolving autonomous AI agents. It allows LLMs to control local computer systems through a minimal set of tools and an agent loop, automatically learning and growing its capabilities into a personal skill tree with each task it completes. Key Takeaways: 💡 ✅ GenericAgent empowers LLMs with full system-level control over local computers using a minimal set of 9 atomic tools. ✅ It features a unique self-evolution mechanism, automatically learning and crystallizing successful task executions into reusable "Skills." ✅ The framework boasts an incredibly minimal architecture (~3K lines of code, ~100-line Agent Loop), ensuring low overhead and easy integration. ✅ GenericAgent is highly token-efficient, operating with a small context window (<30K), which leads to lower costs, fewer hallucinations, and higher success rates. ✅ It offers high compatibility with major LLMs (Claude, Gemini, Kimi, etc.) and cross-platform support, m
author: GitHubOpenSource
summary_zh: 介绍 GenericAgent——一个极简的自进化自主 AI Agent Python 框架，用约 9 个原子工具让 LLM 控制本地计算机，并通过 100 行左右的 Agent Loop 把成功任务自动固化为可复用的「技能树」。整体约 3K 行代码、token 占用低，属于轻量级 Agent 框架的概念展示。
